title: Fix RawFileSlice idx and tags in lexer_test
body:
While running the lexer test in rust mode, these `RawFileSlice` tags came across with type errors. This fixes the order of the `block_idx` and `tag`.

None

(Tests included / updated accordingly.)

step_plan.summary: Correct the order of block_idx and tag in RawFileSlice positional calls inside the lexer slicing test so the tests match the RawFileSlice signature (raw, slice_type, source_idx, block_idx, tag) and eliminate type errors.

steps.operation: Fix RawFileSlice positional arguments in test parametrization
steps.reason: RawFileSlice is defined as (raw, slice_type, source_idx, block_idx, tag). Several parametrized test cases used five positional args with block_idx and tag swapped (passing None for block_idx and an int for tag), causing type mismatches in strict type-checking and test runs. Swap the 4th and 5th positional arguments in each RawFileSlice(...) call so block_idx (int) is the 4th and tag (Optional[str]) is the 5th.
steps.side_effects: Only test code is modified; no production API changes. Running the updated tests should remove the type errors in this test module. Possible impacts: other tests that (incorrectly) relied on the previous mistaken ordering will now reflect the correct semantics; if similar incorrect usages exist elsewhere, they will still fail until fixed. Must run pytest test/core/parser/lexer_test.py and then the broader test suite to ensure no additional occurrences cause failures. No runtime API break for production code, but test expectations (if any asserted on tag vs. block_idx) will now match the intended values.
